description: In South Australia, learner, provisional and probationary drivers must have zero BAC when driving.
In wet weather, slow down and leave extra space because stopping distance increases and visibility may be reduced.
You must slow to 25 km/h when passing a stationary emergency service vehicle displaying flashing red or blue lights.
Do not drive if illness, fatigue, alcohol, drugs or medication could affect your ability to drive safely.
Scan well ahead and around your vehicle so you can identify hazards early and respond safely.
Give trucks and buses extra room, avoid blind spots and allow more time when sharing the road with heavy vehicles.
Loads and trailers must be safe, secure and within legal vehicle and towing limits.
A NO U-TURN sign means you must not make a U-turn at that location.
Unless signs allow it, you must not stop or park within 20 metres of traffic lights or within 10 metres of an intersection without traffic lights.
When turning at an intersection, you must give way to pedestrians crossing the road you are entering.
If required by police, you must comply with alcohol testing; refusing a breath or blood sample can lead to serious penalties.
In South Australia, you must drive at 25 km/h in school zones when children are present.
At a stop sign or stop line, you must come to a complete stop and give way as required before proceeding.
In wet conditions, increase your following distance to allow for reduced grip and longer stopping distances.
Do not follow trucks closely, as it limits your view ahead and reduces the space needed to stop safely.
When roadworks signs or workers are present, slow down, obey the posted speed limit and drive with caution.
Only permitted vehicles may drive in a bus lane unless signs or road rules allow another vehicle to enter briefly.
Loading zones are only for vehicles allowed by the sign, and misuse may result in a fine.
